title: Update (U)
text: Update (U) Update an existing record. Description All fields in the record must be updated. There is no information available about which fields have been modified. For SQL-like DBMSs, the connector must use the ‘if’ list. This identifies exactly one record by either the primary key or the PRA, and optionally a U_VERSION field. For ISAM-like or record-level DBMSs, a Fetch mode 2 has prepared locking and positioning. The positioned record is the record to be updated. Build the concatenated primary key, if required. From Uniface Preconditions realized by Uniface—the table that the Update is requested for is open. Table 1. Information Available from Uniface Data Structure->Element Description uctrl->TMEnabled Indicates whether the connector is TM-controlled. uctrl->udrv Points to the connector workspace if a workspace was requested in the Info request. uctrl->udrvini Unique identification of the current logon path. uctrl->iobuf Points to the new data for the record. uctrl->udatlen Length of the data pointed to by uctrl->iobuf . uctrl->ufldlst Field list that describes all the fields in the table. uctrl->uiflst Identifies the record to be updated. uctrl->uilist Points to the index list. uctrl->vsfnr If vsfnr equals 0, segmented fields do not have to be updated, otherwise they do have to be updated. To Uniface The concatenated primary key must be added to the hitlist by means of the uaddhit service function. If the DBMS supports PRAs and the PRA of the record changed as a result of the Update, the new PRA must be added to the hitlist by means of the uaddhit service function. Table 2. Connector Request Return Values Value Description 0 Exactly one record was updated successfully. -2 Specified record does not exist. -6 Exceptional error. -9 I/O server logon error. -16 DBMS reported network error. When the connector request return value is less than 0, an error code must be supplied in uctrl->udrverr . When more than one record or zero records have been updated, the connector must return an error. Service functions ubldprim —build the primary key in user-supplied buffer. ubldprm —build the primary key in the SQL buffer. Extra information Uniface assumes that the DBMS checks the uniqueness of keys in unique indexes (Uniface primary key and candidate keys). When the connector has to update segmented fields ( vsfnr is not equal to 0) the segments of the segmented field have to be obtained directly from the Uniface kernel using the ugetblob service function. TM-Controlled Connectors TM-controlled connectors are not allowed to begin, commit, or rollback transactions. Related Links uctrl Exchanges general information between Uniface and the connector, and describes a table. ugetblob Get segmented data from the Uniface kernel. TM-Controlled DBMS Connectors A database connector can be controlled by a transaction manager only if it is TM-capable. Uniface does not provide TM-controlled connectors and does not directly support third-party transaction managers. However, you can use the APIs provided by the Database Connector Interface to build a custom database connector that does. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
